heading: Studies of productivity and labor costs in industries
label: 2b Studies of productivity and labor costs in industries
text: The Bureau of Labor Statistics of the United States Department of Labor is authorized and directed to make continuing studies of productivity and labor costs in the manufacturing, mining, transportation, distribution, and other industries.
notes: CodificationProvision of this section authorizing appropriations of up to $100,000 for studies by the bureau in the first fiscal year was omitted.
Amendments1954—Act , repealed second par. which required Secretary of Labor to submit annually to Congress reports of findings under this section.Aug. 30, 1954

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of Labor, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of Labor, with power to delegate, see Reorg. Plan No. 6 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, 15 F.R. 3174, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.64 Stat. 1263
